response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial/GIS background (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/sec). However, the role's primary requirement is modern commercial C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C/C++ exposure is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis) with no Rust experience. Their core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang. The strong domain fit and demonstrated systems/performance instincts make them worth a screening conversation, but the C++ gap against a C++-primary role is significant.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS and mapping data experience
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong craftsmanship and adaptability across languages and the full stack
concerns: Modern commercial C++ experience is limited and dated (~2014–2015)
No Rust experience, the acceptable alternative language
No navigation/routing systems background; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang
